title: Slow Cured Canadian Salmon Pilaf
description: A sour, tender, and indulgent Canadian dish featuring whole wheat and shio-cure, suitable for diabetic-friendly diets. This recipe combines the richness of salmon with the earthiness of whole wheat and the brightness of citrus.
instructions: Rinse the whole wheat berries and soak them in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked wheat berries, then cook them according to package instructions until tender.
In a small bowl, mix together the shio salt, brown sugar, and black pepper to create the cure.
Place the salmon fillets on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet or a tray, and sprinkle the cure evenly over both sides of the fish.
Let the salmon cure in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Rinse the cured salmon fillets under cold running water to remove the cure, then pat them dry with paper towels.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat, then sear the salmon fillets until browned on both sides.
Add the cooked whole wheat berries, lemon juice, and dried dill weed to the skillet, then stir to combine.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through.
